title: Feature flags have operational risk
description: Flags add state, targeting, monitoring, cleanup, and compatibility risks; they are not free safety.
body: Use when a feature flag is introduced or modified. Do not reject flags; use them with explicit controls. Actions: test both states, keep default conservative, define owner and cleanup, and ensure observability for enabled cohorts. Validation signals include both-path tests and monitoring plan. Risk boundary: broad enablement or ambiguous owner requires human approval. Use when Production Change Risk Controls work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
